questions.question: Which city is the host of Bharat Electricity Summit 2026?
questions.explanation: Bharat Electricity Summit 2026 will be held from 19-22 March 2026 at Yashobhoomi, New Delhi. The four-day Summit will focus on sustainable energy systems and India’s role in the global energy transition. The theme of summit is “Electrifying Growth. Empowering Sustainability. Connecting Globally”. It aims to bring together government, industry, academia, and civil society to discuss challenges and opportunities in the power sector. The event features 50+ high-level sessions, expert panels, thematic pavilions, and technology showcases.

questions.question: Which state government has launched ‘Gruha Lakshmi Digital Marketing’ Portal to promote women entrepreneurs?
questions.explanation: Karnataka Department of Women and Child Development has launched Gruha Lakshmi Digital Marketing to promote women entrepreneurs. It is a state-backed e-commerce portal showcasing women-made products to national and global markets. The initiative aims to boost women’s economic independence and digital inclusion. The portal highlights products from urban and rural women, including artisans and small businesses. Only verified women entrepreneurs are onboarded to ensure trust and quality. It offers a simple, customer-friendly digital interface for buyers.

questions.question: Who has been appointed as Vigilance Commissioner in the Central Vigilance Commission?
questions.explanation: Recently, former Special Secretary (Internal Security), Ministry of Home Affairs, Praveen Vashista, was appointed Vigilance Commissioner in the Central Vigilance Commission. He is a 1991-batch Indian Police Service (IPS) officer of the Bihar cadre. He took voluntary retirement before his scheduled superannuation in July. The appointment was made by the President of India under the Central Vigilance Commission Act, 2003. The Central Vigilance Commission consists of one Central Vigilance Commissioner and two Vigilance Commissioners.

questions.question: Bor Tiger Reserve is located in which state?
questions.explanation: Recently, two new safari gates were inaugurated at the Bor Tiger Project in Maharashtra. Bor Tiger Reserve is located in the Wardha district of Maharashtra. It is the smallest tiger reserve in India in terms of area. The reserve covers about 138.12 square kilometres, including the drainage basin of the Bor Dam. It holds strategic importance as it is centrally located among major Bengal tiger habitats.

questions.question: Which state has India’s largest population of saltwater crocodiles in natural habitat?
questions.explanation: A drone-based survey by the Odisha Forest and Environment Department shows that the saltwater crocodile population is increasing. The saltwater crocodile is the largest and heaviest living reptile and is also known as the estuarine or Indo-Pacific crocodile. It lives in saltwater, brackish wetlands, rivers, and mangroves. The species is found along India’s east coast, Southeast Asia, northern Australia, and Micronesia. In India, it occurs in Odisha, West Bengal, and the Andaman and Nicobar Islands, with Odisha having the highest population in natural habitat. It is classified as Least Concern by the International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN).

questions.question: What is the name of the scheme launched by Indian Railways to promote local products at railway stations?
questions.explanation: Indian Railways’ One Station One Product (OSOP) initiative promotes local craftsmanship and grassroots entrepreneurship. Launched in 2022, OSOP transforms railway stations into showcases of India’s regional identity. As of January 19, 2026, 2,002 stations host 2,326 operational OSOP outlets. The scheme benefits over 1.32 lakh (132,000) artisans, weavers, and small producers. Outlets provide direct market access to millions of railway passengers daily. It aligns with the government’s “Vocal for Local” vision and enhances passenger experience.

questions.question: The Punjab government has opened its first dog sanctuary in which city?
questions.explanation: Punjab has opened its first Dog Sanctuary in Ludhiana as a pilot project. The initiative will be replicated across the state. The sanctuary aims to reduce dog bite cases in the city. It will operate according to Supreme Court guidelines.

questions.question: Which day is observed as Kokborok Day in Tripura?
questions.explanation: Tripura observed the 48th Kokborok Day, also known as Tripuri Language Day, with state-wide cultural celebrations. Kokborok Day is celebrated on January 19 annually. The day marks January 19, 1979, when Kokborok was recognized as an official state language. The government is introducing Kokborok courses in colleges and universities. Efforts are being made to strengthen harmony between tribal and non-tribal communities. Kokborok belongs to the Tibeto-Burman language family and is spoken by the Borok (Tripuri) people.

questions.question: Which organization released the report on “Global Water Bankruptcy 2026”?

options.label: B
options.text: UN University Institute for Water, Environment and Health

answer.label: B
answer.text: UN University Institute for Water, Environment and Health

questions.explanation: The United Nations University Institute for Water, Environment and Health released a report on Global Water Bankruptcy. It warns that the planet has entered a Global Water Bankruptcy era. Water Bankruptcy means long-term water use has exceeded renewable supply and safe limits, causing irreversible damage. Rivers, lakes, aquifers, wetlands, soils, and glaciers are degraded beyond full recovery. This differs from water stress, which is high demand but largely reversible. It also differs from water crisis, which is temporary and manageable with restoration. Key causes include chronic overuse of water resources, dam-driven overexpansion, ecological degradation, and climate change intensifying existing water stress.

questions.question: Where was the 9th International Conference on Law and Economics held?
questions.explanation: The Centre for Trade and Investment Law (CTIL), in collaboration with Gujarat National Law University (GNLU), organised the 9th International Conference on Law and Economics at GNLU, Gandhinagar. The Conference focused on empirical and applied law and economics, governance, trade policy, and inclusive public policy. It featured panel discussions, technical sessions, and plenary talks over three days, bringing together academics, judiciary members, industry leaders, and policymakers.
